Entry Of Final Judgment
Off Calendar Oct-04-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Eric R. Fleming, Clerk: A. Mok. Reporter: Mitch Lyon, CSR#6840.

Charlie W. Yu, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff, Jason Yan-Chun Lee and Joice Victoria Lee,
No appearance by defaulted defendants Shirley Naomi Lee or Betty Lan Yuen Lee,
Interlocutory Judgment entered June 19, 2018.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules as follows:
(1) The subject property is located at 5544 Diamond Heights Boulevard, San Francisco, CA 94121. The subject property shall be partitioned by sale;
(2) The subject property is owned by plaintiffs Jason Yan-Chun and Joice Victoria Lee, and defendant Shirley Naomi Lee as tenants-in-common, with plaintiffs Jason Yan-Chun and Joice Victoria holding an undivided 50% interest and defendant Shirley Naomi Lee holding an undivided 50% interest;
(3) Pursuant to CCP 873.010, the Court hereby appoints Kevin Singer Referee, with full authority to manage and partition by sale of the subject property;
(4) Pursuant to CCP 863.510, Referee shall sell the property, and seek court confirmation of the sale. Sale of property shall be provided by CCP 873.600-873.690;
(5) The Referee is authorized to offer the property for sale vacant at closing by taking all actions necessary to receive the highest bide for the sale of the Property;
(6) Upon confirmation of the sale of the subject property by this Court and the payment of the purchase price thereof, the Referee is hereby authorized and directed to execute and deliver a deed of the real property sold to the purchaser;
(7) All reasonable expenses incurred from hiring and retention of authorized personnel and counsel shall be expenses of the property and shall be paid from the proceeds from the sale of the property;
(8) The proceeds from the sale of the Property shall be applied, after confirmation of the sale, to pay the expense of the sale including the court referee; all known lien holders; to pay other costs of partition or secure any cost of partition later allowed; and the residue is to be distributed among the parties in proportion to their interests;
(9) The Referee is authorized to hire employ, retain, and terminate all professional personnel or employees which Referee deems necessary to assist him in the discharge of his duties, and enter into and execute listing and brokerage agreements necessary for the management, listing, and sale of the Property;
(10) The Referee may institute and prosecute all suits as may be reasonably necessary in the Referee's judgment to protect the Properties, and to defend all such suits;
(11) The Referee shall maintain adequate insurance over the Property and to cause all existing policies to be amended by adding the Referee as an additional insured;
(12) The Referee and any party that appeared in this action may petition this court by ex parte or noticed motion for instructions;
(13) No later than sixty (60) days after the Referee's duties hereunder terminate, the Referee shall file and a serve a motion for approval of the Referee's final report and account and exoneration of the Referee's bond;
(14) This Court's final order for distribution of the proceeds from the sales of the Properties and the monies received by the Referee during the Referee's administration, shall include allowances for the costs of partition (including attorney's fees);
(15) The Referee shall keep the proceeds of the sale of his trust account;
(16) The Parties shall fully cooperate with the Referee including completing a Statement of Information and Real Estate Transfer Documents.
(17) This Court shall retain jurisdiction over this action to enforce the Final Judgment.

Final Judgment for Partition signed in open court.

Entry Of Final Judgment

Progress Report On The Appointment Of The Referee And Status Of The Sale Of The Subject Property
Off Calendar Jul-11-2018 Off Calendar

Declaration Of Charlie W. Yu
Off Calendar Jun-19-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514. Judge: Leslie C. Nichols. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, Reporter: Mitch L. Lyon, CSR #6840. Telephone: 415-551-3788.

Charlie W. Yu, Esq. appeared with plaintiffs Jason Yan-Chun Lee and Joice Lee, who were placed under oath and testified.
No appearance by defendants Shirley Naomi Lee or Betty Lan Yuen Lee.
Plaintiffs' complaint to Quiet Title was filed on May 26, 2017.
Notice of Pendancy of Action was filed on 8/11/17.
Order for Publication of Summons as to defendants Shirley Naomi Lee or Betty Lan Yuen Lee was filed on October 16, 2017.
Proof of Publication of Summons as to defendants Shirley Naomi Lee or Betty Lan Yuen Lee was filed on November 16, 2017 (Los Angeles Daily Journal) and on December 8, 2017 (San Francisco Dail Journal).
Default as to defendants Shirley Naomi Lee and Betty Lan Yuen Lee was filed on January 26, 2018.
DOES defendants were dismissed on April 18, 2018.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules as follows:

(1) The subject property is located at 5544 Diamond Heights Boulevard, San Francisco, CA 94121. The subject property shall be partioned by sale;

(2) The subject property is owned by plaintiffs Jason Yan-Chun and Joice Victoria Lee, and defendant Shirley Naomi Lee as tenants-in-common, with plaintiffs Jason Yan-Chun and Joice Victoria holding an undivided one-half interest and defendant Shirley Naomi Lee holding an undivided one-half interest;

(3) Pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure Section 873.010, the Court hereby appoints Kevin Singer Referee, with full authority to manage and partition by sale of the subject property.

(4) Pursuant to Code of Cili Procedure Section 863.510, Referree is directed to submit a report to the Court recommending the proposed method of sale, taking into account what method will be the most beneficial to the parties. The report is to be submitted to the Court and served on all parties. Any party objecting to the proposed method of sale shall file such objections with the Court and serve on the parties within ten (10) days of service of the report;

(5) The Referee is authorized to place the subject property for sale on the market and thereafter sell the subject property in the form and manner approved by the Court in accordance with Code of Civil Procedure Section 873.510;

(6) Upon confirmation of the sale of the subject property by this Court and the payment of the purchase price thereof, the Referee is herby authorized and directed to execute and deliver a deed of the real property sold to the purcherser; and

(7) No later than sixty (60) days after the Referee's duties hereunder terminate, the Referee shall file and a serve a motion for approval of the Referee's final report and account and exoneration of the Referee's bond.

Matter is CONTINUED to Thursday, July 12, 2018 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 514 for Progress Report on the appointment of the Referee and status of the sale of the subject property.

Matter is CONTINUED to September 20, 2018 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 514 for Entry of Final Judgment.

Proposed Interlocutory Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.
